title: He Won $100, Reinvested $5, and Got Back $100,000
script: Win $100, then win HOW? William Jackson of Stuarts Draft, Virginia, stopped at the Food Lion on Stuarts Draft Highway. Bought a scratch-off. Won $100. Standard win — most people pocket it and leave. Jackson did not leave. He took $5 of that $100 and bought another ticket — a Cash Wheel scratcher from the Virginia Lottery. He scratched it right there. $100,000. The whole strategy was: win once, reinvest $5, win 1,000 times more. That's it. Jackson confirmed the prize June 15. He told the lottery he was in complete shock. The Food Lion on that highway now has 2 major winners connected to its ticket machine. So next time you're up $100 on a scratch-off — are you walking out, or buying one more?
